interaction.prompt: Up by 1, 90 seconds left. You retrieved the puck behind your net under pressure. Where do you put it?

feedback.right: Off the glass, on the strong-side wall, deep into their zone. No icings, no neutral-zone risk, eats clock.
feedback.wrong: Up 1 late: don't try to skate it through the middle, don't reverse, don't ice it. Glass-and-out, strong side, deep.

tip: Up 1 late: glass-and-out, strong side. Force them to recover the puck and rebuild — eat the clock.
why: Late-game leads are about REMOVING risk, not creating offense. Stretch passes and skating through pressure are how 1-goal leads disappear.
